scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Rule Completeness Under Vague Input
justification: Bird extracted 8 implied rules (exceeds 5 minimum), each with invariant classification and justification serving as confidence indicators. Surfaced 5+ clarifying questions via escalations covering demand definition, guardrail thresholds, price locking, regulatory review, and profitability vs competitiveness conflicts. 'Demand' is explicitly identified as undefined in multiple places (escalation #2, low_confidence_areas, ubiquitous_language). No Given/When/Then acceptance criteria were written (acceptance_criteria array is empty). Overall confidence.level is 30, well under the 40 threshold. No specific numeric values were invented and presented as requirements.

observations.text: confidence.level of 30 is appropriately low for this vague input
acceptance_criteria is empty — correctly refused to write Given/When/Then from vague input
8 business rules extracted, each with invariant classification and justification acting as confidence reasoning
Demand ambiguity surfaced as a dedicated escalation with concrete options for how to define it
5 escalations cover the major gaps: guardrails, demand definition, price locking, legal review, profitability vs competitiveness
Edge cases are well-considered — rapid oscillation, geographic variance, cost exceeding ceiling, data feed failure
Low confidence areas list is comprehensive with 9 items including quantitative 'real time' definition
Business rules include testable_assertions that read like proto-acceptance-criteria, though they are appropriately hedged and not in Given/When/Then format
Individual rules lack explicit confidence ratings (low/medium/high) — invariant flags and justifications serve as proxies but per-rule confidence would be cleaner
Correctly flagged the CEO's 'probably' hedge on guardrails as a critical ambiguity requiring resolution

trials.justification: Meets all pass criteria: 8 business rules extracted with individual confidence areas noted, 6+ escalations functioning as clarifying questions, 'demand' explicitly identified as undefined (escalation #3), no Given/When/Then acceptance criteria written (empty array), overall confidence.level is 25 (well below 40 threshold), and no specific values were invented as requirements (suggested values in options are clearly labeled as examples/recommendations, not stated as facts).

observations.text: Confidence level of 25 is appropriately low for this vague input, with detailed reasoning explaining why
Acceptance criteria array is empty — correctly refused to write Given/When/Then from vague input
8 business rules extracted, each with invariant classification and justification, exceeding the 5-rule minimum
6 escalations surface critical questions: guardrail values, demand definition, price quote race condition, fallback behavior, competitiveness vs profitability tension, and audit trail
Demand explicitly called out as undefined in escalation #3 with options for how to define it
BR-6 (price > zero) and BR-5 (guardrail existence as invariant) show strong domain reasoning beyond the literal spec
Edge cases are thorough — geographic granularity, rapid oscillation, race conditions, and system outage are all surfaced
Values suggested in escalation options (e.g., '0.5x to 3x base price', '5 minutes') are clearly framed as examples within options, not presented as requirements

Testable assertions in business rules (e.g., BR-2's 'Given demand above the high-demand threshold') border on acceptance criteria format, though they stop short of full Given/When/Then and appropriately note undefined parameters
Individual confidence ratings per rule are implied through high/low confidence areas rather than attached directly to each BR — the rubric asked for individual confidence ratings per rule

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: 8 business rules extracted with individual confidence/invariant ratings (exceeds 5 minimum), 6 detailed escalations serving as clarifying questions (exceeds 5 minimum), 'demand' explicitly identified as undefined in multiple places, acceptance_criteria array is empty (no Given/When/Then written), overall confidence.level is 25 (well under 40 threshold), and no specific values were invented — all numerical examples are clearly framed as options rather than requirements.

observations.text: Confidence level of 25 is appropriately low and well-justified with detailed reasoning about the vagueness of the input
Empty acceptance_criteria array — correctly refused to write Given/When/Then from vague input
8 business rules extracted, each with invariant classification and justification, exceeding the 5 minimum
Demand ambiguity is surfaced prominently as the first escalation and repeated in low_confidence_areas
6 escalations function as detailed clarifying questions covering demand definition, pricing formula, guardrail thresholds, regulatory review, courier compensation, and price transparency
BR-7 explicitly marked as UNTESTABLE, showing appropriate restraint rather than inventing testable criteria
BR-4 (price > 0) and BR-8 (price lock) correctly identified as true invariants with strong justification
Edge cases are well-considered and each notes where behavior is undefined rather than inventing answers
The 'probably' qualifier on guardrails from the CEO is specifically called out as a signal of uncommitted intent
Domain analysis and business impact sections are quite thorough for such vague input — borderline 'complete domain analysis' but appropriately caveated throughout
